blueprint_refs: "simulation_evaluation": metrics: stop_count_mae, category_mix_jsd, time_of_day_emd, poi_topk_recall, radius_of_gyration_error

evidence.metrics: SIMULATION RESULTS report only {category_share_mae, stop_count_abs_mean_error, stop_count_kl, tod_jsd_avg, topk_poi_recall, transition_divergence, trip_distance_wasserstein}; blueprint-required metrics are not produced as top-level metrics.

blueprint-metric-mismatch-missing-requir.error_identification: Evaluation is not aligned to the blueprint’s required metric set and naming (e.g., stop_count_mae vs stop_count_abs_mean_error, category_mix_jsd vs category_share_mae, time_of_day_emd not exposed, radius_of_gyration_error not exposed as a metric).
blueprint-metric-mismatch-missing-requir.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator computes additional blueprint metrics only inside diagnostics (blueprint_metrics block) and uses non-blueprint metric names for the optimization/evaluation surface, so the run cannot be judged/calibrated strictly per blueprint outputs.
blueprint-metric-mismatch-missing-requir.correct_approach: Expose blueprint metrics as first-class outputs and (optionally) as calibration objective terms: (1) Add keys stop_count_mae, category_mix_jsd, time_of_day_emd, poi_topk_recall, radius_of_gyration_error to the returned metric dict. (2) Either rename/alias existing metrics or provide both names. (3) Update objective_weights to reference blueprint names (or map internally). Keep V_test evaluation single-shot as currently done.
blueprint-metric-mismatch-missing-requir.key_insight: Treat metric names as API: if the spec lists metrics, compute them explicitly with exact names and avoid burying them only in diagnostics.

blueprint_refs: "baseline_poi_choice_model": Given chosen category, choose POI via mixture of personal popularity and distance-decay (conditioned on category and distance).
"action_decision_policy": sample activity categories then choose POI conditioned on category
poi-topk-recall-near-zero-due-to-uncondi.error_identification: The simulator almost never reproduces ground-truth POIs (recall@5 ≈ 0.35%).
poi-topk-recall-near-zero-due-to-uncondi.root_cause_analysis: POI selection is not actually conditioned on the chosen activity category/super-category. In Resident.simulate_day, candidates are taken from user/global top POIs *by super-category*, but the scoring weights (pref_w) use baseline.poi_pref and global_poi_pref that are not category-specific; this lets globally popular POIs dominate within a supercat and makes exact POI matching extremely unlikely. Additionally, there is no explicit anchoring to high-frequency per-user POIs per (super)category beyond a shallow top list, and no mechanism to reuse yesterday’s frequent POIs in 2021 context beyond start_poi.
poi-topk-recall-near-zero-due-to-uncondi.correct_approach: Make POI choice explicitly conditional and sharper: (1) Fit per-user per-supercat POI preference distributions from 2019–2020 (counts restricted to that supercat) and use them as the preference component instead of unconditional baseline.poi_pref. (2) In simulate_day, restrict candidates to the chosen supercat’s POI catalog when available (dataset.poi_tokens_by_supercat[sc]) with a cap + sampling, then apply mixture of (user_supercat_pref vs distance-decay). (3) Add an ‘anchor POI reuse’ probability to reselect from the user’s top-K POIs for that supercat, improving recall.
poi-topk-recall-near-zero-due-to-uncondi.key_insight: If the evaluation includes exact-POI overlap, POI sampling must be conditioned (category → POI) and should include a high-mass component on the user’s frequent POIs within that category.

blueprint_refs: "stop_count_mae": MAE between simulated and ground-truth number of visits per (user, day).
"theta_stop_count_multiplier": scales expected number of daily stops
stop-count-error-high-from-mean-matching.error_identification: Daily visit counts are off by ~1.7 visits on average and the count distribution is noticeably mismatched.
stop-count-error-high-from-mean-matching.root_cause_analysis: Stop count sampling targets a shifted mean via an ad-hoc exponential penalty around target (mean_base*mult), but does not re-parameterize or refit the distribution (e.g., via scaling in a parametric family or via resampling with calibrated temperature). This can systematically misfit heavier tails typical in abnormal periods. Also, stop_count_multiplier is globally applied; blueprint allows per-user baseline but expects calibrated abnormal shift parameters to produce good match on 2021.
stop-count-error-high-from-mean-matching.correct_approach: Replace the mean-matching heuristic with a distribution-shaping mechanism: (1) compute a per-daytype baseline count PMF support, then apply a calibrated temperature/tilt: w(c) ∝ pmf(c) * exp(λ*c) where λ is derived from theta_stop_count_multiplier or directly calibrated; normalize. (2) Optionally allow separate multipliers for weekday/weekend or per-user offset term if calibration is still global. (3) Clip support by empirical 2021 plausible range per user (from V_calib only) without using test data.
stop-count-error-high-from-mean-matching.key_insight: To match count MAE and count KL simultaneously, use a principled exponential tilt/temperature transform of the baseline PMF rather than shifting only the mean with arbitrary penalties.

blueprint_refs: "time_step": minutes; output HH:MM:SS by rounding/stochastic seconds jitter
"time_of_day_emd": EMD between simulated vs ground-truth visit times (minutes-of-day)
time-of-day-mismatch-from-30min-binning-.error_identification: Time-of-day distributions show a moderate divergence (JSD ~0.058), suggesting schedules are somewhat misaligned.
time-of-day-mismatch-from-30min-binning-.root_cause_analysis: Start times are sampled from a coarse 30-minute histogram, and subsequent visit times are driven by unconditioned gap samples (per daytype only) with a hard end-of-day clamp; there is no conditioning on time-of-day, activity type, or stop index. This tends to compress or smear the intra-day temporal structure and can shift mass toward late-day boundary conditions.
time-of-day-mismatch-from-30min-binning-.correct_approach: Increase temporal fidelity and add simple structure: (1) reduce TIME_BIN_MINUTES to 10 or 15 for start time histograms, or fit a KDE-like discrete distribution over minutes-of-day from training. (2) Condition gap sampling on stop index (early/mid/late) and/or on super-category transition (prev_sc→next_sc) using training-derived gap samples. (3) When clamping to end-of-day, resample gaps/stop_count rather than repeatedly forcing events into the last minutes.
time-of-day-mismatch-from-30min-binning-.key_insight: Coarse start-time bins plus unconditioned gaps usually produce correct-looking averages but fail distributional metrics; add conditioning on stop index/transition to recover realistic temporal structure.

blueprint_refs: "category_mix_jsd": JSD between simulated vs ground-truth distributions of fine categories (or super-categories)

evidence.metrics: category_share_mae=0.027798996902778872 (not JSD; computed on super-categories via catto mapping).

category-mix-metric-not-jsd-and-uses-sup.error_identification: Reported category metric is MAE on super-category shares rather than the blueprint’s category_mix_jsd (fine or super).
category-mix-metric-not-jsd-and-uses-sup.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator computes category_share_mae as the primary category metric and computes category_mix_jsd only inside diagnostics. This changes both optimization pressure and reported performance compared to the blueprint requirement.
category-mix-metric-not-jsd-and-uses-sup.correct_approach: Compute and surface category_mix_jsd as a first-class metric (fine-category and/or super-category variant) and use it in the objective. If keeping category_share_mae as an auxiliary metric, report both and pick one explicitly for calibration per blueprint.
category-mix-metric-not-jsd-and-uses-sup.key_insight: When a spec says “JSD”, using MAE instead can hide mode-collapse/mass-shift errors; match the divergence family required by the evaluation.

blueprint_refs: "radius_of_gyration_error": requires POI lat/lon; handle missing POIs by excluding those visits
"baseline_poi_choice_model": distance-decay from current POI using lat/lon
distance-missingness-discrepancy-masks-s.error_identification: Ground-truth step distances are mostly missing coordinates while simulated steps are mostly scorable; this creates an apples-to-oranges comparison for spatial distance distributions and can distort the reported distance metric.
distance-missingness-discrepancy-masks-s.root_cause_analysis: Simulation tends to pick POIs from the POI catalog (which has coordinates), while ground truth contains many POI tokens not found in the catalog or not normalized to match, leading to high missingness in gt distance computations. The evaluator computes Wasserstein on the remaining gt distances without adjusting for missingness bias.
distance-missingness-discrepancy-masks-s.correct_approach: Reduce token mismatch and correct comparison bias: (1) strengthen normalization/aliasing of POI tokens by building a more robust mapping (e.g., stripping punctuation already done; additionally consider case normalization and consistent whitespace) using dataset.poi_token_by_normalized in both parsing and evaluation. (2) In parse_1921y_records, resolve tokens via norm_index at parse time so gt tokens align with catalog when possible. (3) For trip distance metric, report missingness-adjusted metric: either compute on intersection-only days/steps (both gt and sim have coords) or weight by shared-coordinate fraction; at minimum, log and threshold missingness to warn that the metric is unreliable.
distance-missingness-discrepancy-masks-s.key_insight: Spatial metrics are only meaningful when coordinate coverage is comparable; normalize tokens early and compute distance metrics on matched-coordinate subsets.

blueprint_refs: "Online OOD context window rule": 7-day context window strictly earlier than target; may include earlier 2021 and backfill from late 2020

evidence.metrics: Context is implemented only as last POI seeding; no direct metric isolates this.

no-online-context-window-features-beyond.error_identification: The 7-day context window requirement is only weakly utilized (only the last POI is used), limiting OOD adaptation and personalization in 2021 rollouts.
no-online-context-window-features-beyond.root_cause_analysis: _context_last_poi returns a single token (last POI) rather than a context summary (recent POI frequency, recent category mix, recent stop-count tendencies) that could influence day planning. This underuses the blueprint’s intended online context mechanism.
no-online-context-window-features-beyond.correct_approach: Expand context usage while respecting the strict ‘past-only’ rule: return a compact context object (e.g., last_poi, recent_top_pois, recent_supercat_mix, recent_mean_stop_count, recent_start_time_shift proxy) computed from days < target_date, and feed it into simulate_day to modulate initial supercat distribution, anchor reuse probability, and stop_count sampling.
no-online-context-window-features-beyond.key_insight: If a spec provides an online context window, use it to condition multiple parts of generation (counts, categories, POIs), not just the starting location.
